parameters: Conduit reached. HP and SP fully restored.
You can now configure Elovia's 2nd Skill using the item under the Extra tab or by pressing '2'.
You can now configure Elovia's 3rd Skill using the item under the Extra tab or by pressing '3'.
You can now configure Elovia's 4th Skill using the item under the Extra tab or by pressing '4'.
You can now configure Yurasa's 2nd Skill using the item under the Extra tab or by pressing 'T'.
You can now configure Yurasa's 3rd Skill using the item under the Extra tab or by pressing 'Y'.
You can now configure Yurasa's 4th Skill using the item under the Extra tab or by pressing 'U'.
The \c[27]\i[1]Inferno\c[0] path of Skills focuses on versatility and ease of use, bringing an array of tools that are useful against many types of enemies.
\n\ns[106] is a simple but both effective and reliable tool that will see use against lots of enemy types.
It deals damage twice, with the first hit dealing slightly less damage than the second.
This makes it handy against enemies that have a chance to dodge Skills.
\n\ns[127] is effective against targets that have a lot of S.DEF due to ignoring a portion of it.
It can also be used to finish off low-health enemies that are hiding behind Shield Points or other enemies with Taunt.

\n\ns[142] is a straight buff to all stats, making all the target's actions more effective.
Its long duration makes it even better in harder battles, like against Bosses and Minibosses.
The \c[28]\i[2]Hellfire\c[0] path of Skills focuses on dealing large amounts of damage quickly, but with higher risk.

\n\ns[107] deals damage to all enemies, making it very useful at clearing up the battlefield of weaker enemies.
Elovia gains a boost to AGI specifically when she's using this skill, meaning it can be used to push her forward in the action order for this turn and "intercept" enemies.

\n\ns[128] gives a large boost to Elovia's S.ATK, enhancing her offensive power greatly.
However, she's vulnerable after casting it due to taking 50% more damage from Skills, which can be a huge amount depending on the enemy and Skill.
\n\ns[143] doesn't end Elovia's turn on use, making it very powerful.
It can be used to finish off low-health enemies before they act, stack extra damage onto a tough foe or set up multiple kills for multi-target Skills like \is[107].
The \c[29]\i[3]Hearth\c[0] path of Skills focuses on Elovia tanking damage, but later becomes capable of dealing damage just like the other paths if so desired.

\n<First Skill: \is[108]>\ns[108] doesn't end Elovia's turn on use, meaning it can be combined with any other action the player desires. Guarding while Elovia's under its effect can be particularly powerful, as Elovia draws all attacks to herself before then halving their damage.
\n\ns[129] means Elovia gets healed every time she takes damage, prolonging her time in the fight.
The healing effect is even capable of nullifying attacks that should be lethal, though be wary of poison effects.
It shines the most against fewer enemies or enemies with less / no attacks that deal damage multiple times.
\nThe first damaging Skill of the Hearth set, \ns[144] is very useful for drawing a single enemy to Elovia, weakening them, dealing damage and protecting allies all at once.
It is also influenced more heavily by ATK, which is easier to increase without risk than S.ATK, in its damage.
The \c[14]\i[5]Medic\c[0] path of Skills focuses on recovering health and shielding, but is still capable of doing damage in its own ways.

\n<First Skill: \is[109]>\ns[109] is a reliable source of Health Point recovery, and tends to heal more HP than healing items while additionally not carrying the risk of potentially applying a debuff.

\n\ns[124] is like \is[109], but can be used at the beginning of fights to boost a battler's health past their maximum.
Additionally, this extra health does not need to be healed with Skills or Items once it is damaged.

\n\ns[146] is especially useful against Bosses and other higher-health targets you know you'll be fighting for more than a few turns.
It deals good damage and heals a lot of health over its lifetime.

The \c[15]\i[6]Transcended\c[0] path of Skills is focused on manipulation of status effects, applying powerful Signs on enemies that can severely weaken them. Note: only one sign can be active on an enemy at a time.

\n\ns[110] gives Yurasa an extremely high AGI bonus while using it, meaning it can be used to remove negative status effects at the beginning of the action order.
However, it does not remove status effects with both positive and negative effects, only ones with purely negative ones.
\n\ns[125]'s Sign makes the target take more damage from most sources, and deals damage itself over its lifespan.
This effect can be very powerful against larger enemies.
\n<Third Skill: \is[147]>\ns[147] applies a Sign that severely weakens the affected enemy's Skills, making it very powerful against offensive-orientated enemies.
The \c[26]\i[7]Epoch\c[0] path of Skills essentially focuses on making your team's Skills come out faster than the enemys'. It also has a of damaging skills.

\n\ns[111] deals damage to the target and flings it backwards, meaning it can't use Melee Skills for the next 2 turns.
This is especially powerful against enemies that you know lack non-Melee options in combat.

\n\ns[126] deals damage to an enemy and makes their Ranged Skills take longer to come off cooldown as long as they're cast during its effect's lifespan.
This can be useful for slowing down high-damage enemies, giving you more time to deal with them in other ways.

\n\ns[148] doesn't end Yurasa's turn, making it stronger than SP-restoring items.
It can be especially useful if an ally needs some SP to help finish off a low-health enemy before it has a chance to heal.
Basic robots used by LUCI that serve a variety of purposes.

\is[176]
Ranged single attack where the drone fires a bolt of electricity at a target.

\is[177]
When the Drone is below 50% HP, it will increase its AGI, ATK and S.ATK significantly for \C[8]3 turns\C[0]. Will not be used if the Drone is by itself.
Light but strong, these prototype walkers are an upgrade over Drones in almost every regard.

\is[179]
Melee attack where the Walker charges at a random enemy, dealing medium damage.

\is[180]
Melee attack that deals slight damage many times. Be wary of this Skill if you are currently suffering from debuffs.
Experimental in design, Sentinels support waves of other robots in a variety of ways.

\is[182]
The Sentinel fixes an allied robot, healing it moderately.

\is[183]
The Sentinel marks an enemy, making it take +6 damage from all direct sources for the next \c[8]2-3 turns\c[0]. Destroying the Sentinel removes this effect instantly.
The Sentinel starts out with 50 \c[14]Shield Points\c[0].
Small, annoying robots that harass foes.

\is[189]
The Wasp deals melee damage multiple times to one enemy.

\is[190]
After being damaged slightly, the Wasp has a chance to activate this protocol, and will crash into a low HP enemy the following turn, destroying itself.
Large, aggressive construct that charges into battle, crushing all it encounters with its great maw.

\is[201]
The Devourer marks a single enemy in preparation, then charges at it with a powerful Melee attack next turn.

\is[198]
Melee attack where the Devourer bites a single enemy, recovering HP equal to the damage dealt. Only used when the Devourer is at low HP.

\is[199]
The Devourer enters a frenzied state, countering all Basic Attacks made by it with a powerful attack for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0].
The Devourer starts out with 110 \c[14]Shield Points\c[0].
The Devourer explodes when it dies, dealing heavy damage to all other robots around it.
Gargantuan robots that destroy their foes with large melee attacks.

\is[205]
The Crusher deals great melee damage to the enemy with the highest current HP.

\is[207]
When reaching 50% HP, the Crusher activates this protocol, gaining a moderate amount of \c[14]Shield Points\c[0]. It will then counter all Basic Attacks made against it with a weaker version of \is[205] for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0].
The Crusher takes 50% less damage from Damage over Time effects.
Enigmatic robots that disguise themselves as containers around Arc-Tec, lying in wait for unsuspecting foes.

\is[209]
The Mimic empowers an ally, increasing all stats by 20% for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0]. Destroying the Mimic removes this effect instantly.

\is[210]
The Mimic empowers an ally, decreasing cooldown of all Skills cast by 5 for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0]. Destroying the Mimic removes this effect instantly.
Swift, frail pieces of seemingly mutated plant life.

\is[212]
The Wisteria heals a hurt plantform slightly. Will not use this on other Wisteria.
The Wisteria takes 50% more damage from Fire.
Bulky plantform that attacks with a persistent barrage of heavy strikes.

\is[214]
The Tunicate swings around, dealing melee damage to all enemies once.

\is[215]
After taking a small amount of damage, the Tunicate uses this Skill. Once active, it will gain a grudge against the next foe who targets it.

\is[217]
The Tunicate charges down a foe that is under its grudge, dealing large melee damage to it once.
The Tunicate takes 25% less damage from attacks and Skills that target multiple targets.
The Tunicate takes 25% less damage from attacks and Skills that hit multiple times.
Tumorous plantform that grows in cramped spaces. A faint metallic tang wafts from it constantly.

\is[220]
When the Heimlich is at low HP, it will devour a lesser plantform, healing itself based on the HP of the victim.

\is[221]
After being damaged slightly, the Heimlich will use this Skill. The following turn, it blindly strikes the ground, dealing slight damage to everything except itself multiple times.
The Heimlich takes 50% less damage from attacks and Skills that target multiple targets.
The Heimlich takes 50% less damage from attacks and Skills that hit multiple times.
The Heimlich's S.ATK is increased by 50% when below 50% HP.
A gargantuan robot, heavily damaged from its fall but still incredibly dangerous. Seems to be powered by Dimensional Energy.

\is[226]
The Leviathan shoots a projectile at an enemy, dealing moderate damage once.

\is[227]
The Leviathan shoots a ring of electricity that restrains a foe’s Gift, dealing moderate damage once and locking off 1st, 2nd and 3rd skills for the next \c[8]3-4 turns\c[0]. Guarding protects from this effect.

\is[228]
The Leviathan charges up all its energy. After \c[8]3 turns\c[0], it is released in the form of a devastating attack that will knockout any foe that has not put up some form of defence.
Following this attack, the Leviathan shuts down for the next \c[0]3 turns\c[0].
The Leviathan's AGI is increased by 50% when below 33% HP.
The Leviathan starts out with 150 \c[14]Shield Points\c[0].
What looks like a relative of the Wasp robots, designed to repair the Leviathan from minor damage..

\is[182]
The Repair Drone fixes an allied robot, healing it moderately.
Crude mechanical construct made of Arc-Tec scrap.
Can protect itself from Skills at the expense of becoming more vulnerable to Basic Attacks.

\is[112]
Melee skill that deals medium Slashing damage.
The Walker creates a force field in front of it that makes it take -50% damage from Skills and +30% damage from Basic Attacks for the next \C[8]3 turns\C[0].
Crude mechanical construct made of Arc-Tec scrap, used by LUCI as eyes and alarms.
Capable of overdriving itself to greatly increase its offensive capabilities.

\is[120]
Ranged skill that deals medium Projectile damage.

\is[121]
Deals no damage, but the target has -50% DEF / S.DEF for the next \c[8]4 turns\c[0].

\is[122]
The Drone has +25% ATK, +50% S.ATK and +150% AGI for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0].
Spider-like construct that uses its electrical tendrils to repair allied machines.
Loses effectiveness when it's at low health.

\is[136]
Restores a medium amount of Health Points. \c[8]Cannot target the caster\c[0].

\is[137]
Restores a small amount of Health Points. \c[17]Doesn't end the Sentinel's turn\c[0]. Less likely to be used once the Sentinel is below 50% health.
Annoying flying machine that uses loud noises to distract opponents, drawing attacks away from its allies.
Can create weak shields around itself if it is heavily damaged.

\is[131]
The Wasp draws all attacks to itself for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0].

\is[132]
The Wasp gains a small amount of Shield Points that last for the next \c[8]4 turns\c[0].
The Wasp starts the battle with 60 Shield Points.
Skills have a -10% chance to hit against the Wasp.
Aggressive mechanical construct that rushes down its enemies with heavily disruptive attacks.

\is[98]
The Devourer's Basic Attack is counted as a Melee attack.

\is[117]
Melee attack that deals heavy Biting damage and heals the Devourer's HP \c[8]by 50%\c[0] of the damage dealt.

\is[118]
Melee attack that deals heavy damage. Bypasses all Shield Points. The target also takes extra damage at the end of each turn for the next \c[8]3-5 turns\c[0].
Spindly metal construct that disguises itself as a normal container in order to take its targets off-guard.

\is[156]
Recovers a medium amount of Health Points and Skill Points. \c[8]Cannot target the caster\c[0].

\is[155]
Ranged attack that deals medium damage and heals the Mimic's \c[8]HP by 75%\c[0] of the damage dealt and \c[8]SP by 25%\c[0] of the damage dealt. More likely to be used when the Mimic's below 75% health.

\is[154]
A series of melee attacks that deal slight damage each. More likely to be used when the Mimic's below 50% health.
Curious flying machine that wreathes itself in energy to deliver powerfully disruptive attacks from a distance.

\is[150]
Ranged attack that deals medium damage. The target enemy has -25% ATK / S.ATK and cannot use their 1st skill for the next \c[8]2-4 turns\c[0].

\is[151]
The Pulsar gains +50% ATK, S.ATK and AGI for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0].

\is[152]
A powerful ranged attack that hits all enemies. More likely to be used when the Pulsar is affected by \is[151].
Worn with age, these old security turrets are still capable of raining down death.

\is[173]
Marks the enemy, making them vulnerable to \is[174] for the next \c[8]4 turns\c[0].

\is[174]
The turret fires upon a target that's been previously marked by \is[173], dealing heavy damage. 50% of this damage bypasses Shield Points.

\is[176]
The turret fires randomly, hitting 4 random targets. Only used when the turret's below 35% health.
The Head unit commands the others, speeding up their attacks and debuffing enemies. Damage to this unit may mean the others will cease function temporarily as they reboot.

\is[159]
The target unit gains +20% ATK / S.ATK, and any offensive Skills cast while under this effect have -1 cooldown. Lasts \c[8]2-3 turns\c[0].

\is[160]
Ranged attack that deals slight damage to the target and has a chance of debuffing them, reducing all their stats except HP / SP by 25% for the next \c[8]2-3 turns\c[0].
The Chest unit repairs the others and shields them from harm. Contains volatile chemicals that may detonate after heavy damage, harming nearby units.

\is[166]
Restores a medium amount of Health Points. Cannot target the Chest unit.

\is[165]
Removes negative status effects from the target unit, and adds a small amount of temporary Shield Points to it at the end of each turn for the next \c[8]3-4 turns\c[0].
Restores a medium amount of Skill Points to all units including the Chest, and adds Shield Points to them in addition.
Only used when at least one other unit has been destroyed.
The Leg units bolster the others' speed while dealing quick, disruptive blows to enemies.

\is[179]
The Leg Units' Basic Attack is counted as a Melee attack, and triggers twice.

\is[168]
Melee attack that deals damage twice. Has a chance to critically hit.

\is[169]
The Leg units will completely dodge and counter any Skills or items used against them with \is[170] for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0].

\is[170]
Melee attack that deals damage three times. Has a chance to critically hit.
The Leg units gain +50% ATK and AGI, and +40% S.ATK, for the next \c[8]5 turns\c[0].

All of the Leg units' Skills now have a +25% chance to critically hit.
Only used when at least two other units have been destroyed.
The Weapon unit is the primary source of Modulus' destructive power, capable of dealing damage both from a distance and up close.

\is[163]
Melee attack that attacks five times with low accuracy, each time hitting a random enemy.

Large, aggressive construct that charges into battle, crushing all it encounters with its great maw.

\is[198]
Melee attack where the Devourer bites a single enemy, recovering HP equal to the damage dealt. Only used when the Devourer is at low HP.

\is[199]
The Devourer enters a frenzied state, countering all Basic Attacks made by it with a powerful attack for the next \c[8]3 turns\c[0].

\is[201]
The Devourer marks a single enemy in preparation, then charges at it with a powerful Melee attack next turn.
The Devourer starts out with 110 \c[14]Shield Points\c[0].

\n<--->It matters not who I am,\.\. but what I offer you.
\n<--->Do you see anything else in the mirror?
\n<--->My previous guest was not so pleasant.
\n<--->I used to fear being alone here.\.\. Now, I do not mind the emptiness.
Before you start, you will need to create a layers folder in your project.
This is where all your layer images will be taken from.
Each map can have many graphic layers, but be aware that the more you have,
the more chance the game could lag (especially on slow computers or mobile
When a layer is made, it is stored, so you can set layers for maps before
a player enters them. The layers are created when the map is created, so
for layer commands created on the same map, 'REFRESH LAYERS' plugin command
can be used to force the layers to create during play.
Below are commands to be used in the "Plugin Command" event command.
NOTE: This is different to the "Script" event command.
LAYER MAPID ID GRAPHIC XSPEED YSPEED OPACITY Z XSHIFT YSHIFT BLEND
LAYER_S MAPID ID GRAPHIC X Y OPACITY Z BLEND
To create or change a layer graphic, the following plugin command is used:
LAYER MAPID ID GRAPHIC XSPEED YSPEED OPACITY Z XSHIFT YSHIFT BLEND
Each property is separated by a space and you will exchange the property
LAYER        - do not change this, it is the keyword for the plugin.
MAPID        - the id of the map you are creating the layer for
ID           - the id of the layer itself. If you want to change or remove
- an existing layer, you refer to it by it's id.
GRAPHIC      - The filename of the image found in /img/layers/
XSPEED       - The speed of the horizontal movement. Negatives to go left
YSPEED       - The speed of the vertical movement. Negatives to go up
Z            - What priority the image has. 0 = ground, 5 = above all chars
XSHIFT       - Horizonal movement shift that differs from player movement
YSHIFT       - Vertical movement shift that differs from player movement
- Make XSHIFT and YSHIFT = 0 to move with map
BLEND        - Blend mode (0 = normal, 1 = add, 2 = multiply, 3 = screen)
- *NOTE RPG Maker MV version 1.1 mutliply isn't supported
This will create/change layer 1 on map 12. It will make it use "clouds.png"
as the image that will scroll right, be partially transparent and above all
You can use a variable for any property (except 'GRAPHIC') in the plugin
if you put a "v" before the property. The number after will be variable id.
This is the same example as above, but will use the value of variable 1
for the opacity of the layer. This only changes the setting when called,
changing the variable again without calling this plugin command again will
Alternatively, a static sprite layer can be created. These layers will not
tile/loop and are stuck to the map, better used for parallax mapping:
LAYER_S MAPID ID GRAPHIC X Y OPACITY Z BLEND XANCHOR YANCHOR CHAR ROTATE
Each property is separated by a space and you will exchange the property
LAYER_S      - do not change this, it is the keyword for the plugin.
MAPID        - the id of the map you are creating the layer for
ID           - the id of the layer itself. If you want to change or remove
- an existing layer, you refer to it by it's id.
GRAPHIC      - The filename of the image found in /img/layers/
X            - The X position of the static layer image on the map.
Y            - The Y position of the static layer image on the map.
Z            - What priority the image has. 0 = ground, 5 = above all chars
BLEND        - Blend mode (0 = normal, 1 = add, 2 = multiply, 3 = screen)
- *NOTE RPG Maker MV version 1.1 mutliply isn't supported
XANCHOR      - 0 left, 0.5 middle, 1 right - the part of the image that
- is set to the X position porperty above.
YANCHOR      - 0 left, 0.5 middle, 1 right - the part of the image that
- is set to the Y position porperty above.
CHAR         - 0 for none, -1 for player, above 0 for a specific event id
ROTATE       - 0 for no, 1 for yes, to rotate the sprite depending on the
- direction the specified CHAR is facing. Default graphic
- should be facing down. This doesn't work if no CHAR is set
This will create/change layer 2 on map 6. It will make it use "town.png"
as the image that will stick with the map. The X and Y are 0's which will
be common for most parallax mapping. Z is 0 to appear under the player.
LAYER REMOVE MAPID ID  - Remove a layer from a map
LAYER REMOVE 12 1       - Removes layer 1 from map 12
LAYER REFRESH          - For creating new layers on the same map as the
- plugin command. Do this command if creating
- NEW layers on the map. It is not required for
In addition to using the plugin calls, you can setup layers on each map in
the NOTE section of the map settings. Do this in the same way as the plugin
call except do not add the map ID.
LAYER ID GRAPHIC XSPEED YSPEED OPACITY Z XSHIFT YSHIFT BLEND
LAYER_S ID GRAPHIC X Y OPACITY Z BLEND XANCHOR YANCHOR CHAR ROTATE
These layers can be changed as normal with plugin commands.
For advanced users only. Layer properties can be accessed via script:
"property" being the above properites in lowercase.
Battle layers are controlled with this script call. They don't use the
Plugin Command but the values are the same as the plugin commands above
(without the map ID). Once set, battle layers last for every combat afte

\n<Elovia>Whoever they are, they seem to be well past their glory days...
\n<Yurasa>Can't say I have.\.\. Looks like they've been disbanded for a while now, judging by the state of this place.
\n<Elovia>We haven't seen any other humans so far, yeah.
\n<Elovia>Don't think that guy is down there.\.\. I should check elsewhere first.
\n<Elovia>The elevator's been destroyed by LUCI.\.\. I don't see any other route out.
\n<Yurasa>And what if this is what it \fiwants\fi us to do?
\n<Elovia>I promise we'll make it outta here.
\n<Elovia>(God, what have I gotten us into?)
\n<Elovia>I saw some overgrowth up above too.\.\. Looks like this place has really been abandoned for ages.
On the base, the word \c[8]"DIMENSIONAL"\c[0] can be made out clearly.
The box doesn't seem interested in conversation.
\n<Elovia>No, it's fine. I never liked my nostrils anyway.
\n<Elovia>What do you think this tastes like?
\n<Yurasa>The culmination of... however many years of dampness and general science-y gunk.
\n<Elovia>This doesn't look natural.\.\. Could it be mutated, like us?
It feels as if the sludge is squirming in response to your presence.
A metallic tang emanates from the sludge.
\n<Yurasa>That looked like a lot of fun.
\n<Yurasa>But there's a non-zero chance I won't, right?
\n<Yurasa>They'd throw you all over the place and you'd do a lot of screaming.
\n<Yurasa>T-2 always says he wants to ride one, y'know.
